name: claude-session-sync
description: Sync Claude Code conversation history (and skills/MCP) across machines — via an existing file-sync folder (Syncthing/iCloud/Dropbox/OneDrive/Google Drive) OR self-contained over a git remote (no external sync app needed). Per-project locking prevents simultaneous-access corruption, and an in-use (アクセス中) tab lets you safely disconnect a stale lock from any device (blocked while that conversation is actually running). Auto-titles conversations by content and language. Detects device switches and tells Claude the right working path. The claude -h browser adds favorites, conversation forking, start-new-with-context, resume-with-a-chosen-permission, an in-use tab with remote disconnect, and a separate subagent-history tab; main vs subagent rows are marked with 🤖. claude -h / claude -a work from any shell — PowerShell, cmd.exe and Git Bash on Windows, bash/zsh on macOS/Linux — via PATH shims, so they no longer fall back to the raw help text outside PowerShell. A check-deps doctor verifies required tools (claude CLI, PowerShell/python3) at install and first run. Short launch commands work in every shell — c (launch), cfp (launch in a fixed folder you pick via a native folder dialog), ch (history), ca (settings) — and a per-launch-method remote-control toggle re-enables phone control on launch. An interactive 'claude -a' settings menu sets up auto-starting claude at login — multiple conversations, each with its own model, thinking depth and permission (plan…full-bypass, with warnings) — plus a multi-instance check, sync status, toggles, and restore-to-original. Windows/macOS/Linux.
